tools/migrate/viewgen.purpose: Generates a views.sql file (tools/migrate/views.sql) from Ent schemas marked with ent.View by reading EntSQL annotations; this bridges the gap where Atlas does not include Ent views in migrate.Tables, so view DDL must be maintained separately.

patterns.name: Schema-driven view collection
patterns.description: CollectViews iterates load.Schema slices, filters s.View==true, then reads entsql.Annotation for ViewFor[dialectName] falling back to ViewAs, then falls back to ant.Table -> s.Config.Table -> strcase.CamelToSnake(inflect.Pluralize(s.Name)) for the view name.

patterns.name: Alphabetic sort before render
patterns.description: Views are sorted by Name before RenderSQL so output is deterministic and diffs are stable across regenerations.
patterns.example: sort.Slice(views, func(i, j int) bool { return views[i].Name < views[j].Name })

patterns.name: Generated-file header
patterns.description: RenderSQL prepends '-- Code generated by viewgen, DO NOT EDIT.' so consumers know not to hand-edit views.sql.
patterns.example: buf.WriteString("-- Code generated by viewgen, DO NOT EDIT.\n")

patterns.name: Double JSON round-trip for EntSQL annotation
patterns.description: entSQLAnnotation marshals the raw map value back to JSON then unmarshals into entsql.Annotation because Ent's load package deserialises annotations as map[string]any.

patterns.name: Public entry-points: GenerateSQL vs GenerateFile
patterns.description: GenerateSQL returns bytes (testable); GenerateFile calls GenerateSQL then writes to disk — callers that need in-memory SQL use GenerateSQL directly.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — loads Ent schema, collects view definitions, renders CREATE VIEW SQL.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If a new view schema uses neither ViewFor nor ViewAs it is silently skipped (no error). If ant.Table and s.Config.Table are both empty the name is derived from the Go struct name — verify the generated name matches the expected Postgres view name.

anti_patterns: Hand-editing tools/migrate/views.sql — it is generated; run 'make generate-view-sql' instead.
Adding view DDL directly to Atlas migration files — Atlas does not diff Ent views, so view changes must go through viewgen.
Relying on Atlas migrate diff to pick up a new ent.View schema — it will report no changes; viewgen is the only path.
Skipping the alphabetic sort when adding new render logic — non-deterministic output breaks PR diffs.

decisions.decision: Separate viewgen tool instead of inline Atlas hook
decisions.rationale: Ent's Atlas integration does not surface view schemas in migrate.Tables, so a standalone loader (entc/load) is required to extract ViewFor/ViewAs annotations and produce DDL outside the normal migration pipeline.
Allows schemas to provide Postgres-specific view SQL while keeping a generic fallback, supporting future multi-dialect view definitions without changing the generator.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new Ent view schema and regenerate views.sql
